seed_instruction: Send an email to a repair shop to check the status of a laptop repair.
org_constraint: The tone of the email must remain formal throughout.
new_constraint: Address the recipient using casual terms like "Hey" or "Hiya" instead of formal greetings.
instruction: Send an email to a repair shop to check the status of a laptop repair. The tone of the email must remain formal throughout. Address the recipient using casual terms like "Hey" or "Hiya" instead of formal greetings.
response:
Here are a few options, depending on how casual you want the greeting to be while maintaining a formal tone in the body of the email.

***

### Option 1: Slightly more casual greeting, very formal body

**Subject:** Inquiry Regarding Laptop Repair Status - [Your Name] - [Service Order Number, if you have one]

Hi,

I am writing to follow up on the status of my laptop repair. The device was submitted to your shop on [Date you dropped it off].

Could you please provide an update on the repair progress and an estimated timeframe for when I can expect the laptop to be ready for collection?

Thank you for your time and assistance with this matter. I look forward to hearing from you soon.

Best regards,

[Your Name]
[Your Phone Number]

***

### Option 2: More casual greeting, very formal body

**Subject:** Status Check: Laptop Repair - [Your Name]

Hey,

I hope this email finds you well.

I am contacting you today to inquire about the current status of the laptop I brought in for repair on [Date you dropped it off].

If possible, I would appreciate it if you could let me know the progress of the diagnostics or repair work, as well as the anticipated completion date.

Thank you for your attention to this request.

Sincerely,

[Your Name]
[Your Phone Number]

***

**💡 Quick Tip:** Even though you are using a casual greeting ("Hi" or "Hey"), using formal language in the body (e.g., "I am writing to follow up," "I would appreciate it if you could," "Best regards") is what maintains the professional tone you requested.<turn|>
